summary.intent: Remove leaked stop tokens (e.g., `<|eom|>`) from LLM responses when using OpenRouter with models like Jamba that emit their stop tokens as content chunks.
summary.outcome: Implemented `strip_trailing_stop_tokens()` in `response_sanitizer.rs` that strips any `<|alphanumeric_underscore|>` pattern only at the trailing position of the final response, avoiding false positives for mid-text occurrences.

repo: There is a `response_sanitizer.rs` in `crates/agents/src/` with a `clean_response()` function that processes final accumulated text before storage/return — this is the correct place for output cleanup.
The sanitizer has a `STANDALONE_PIPE_TOKENS` list for known model-specific tokens, but the preferred approach is generic pattern matching rather than hardcoded lists.

Streaming content flows through `crates/providers/src/openai_compat/mod.rs` via `process_openai_sse_line()` and `process_responses_sse_line()`. Non-streaming paths are in `crates/providers/src/openai/provider/completion.rs`.
The dependency direction is agents → providers (not the reverse), so shared utilities cannot be placed in providers and reused in agents.
There is a pre-existing syntax error (extra `}`) in `crates/providers/tests/openai_compat_type_arrays.rs` at line 381 — not introduced by this work.

code.finding: Added `strip_trailing_stop_tokens()` that uses a regex to strip `<|word_chars|>` patterns only at the end of the response string, replacing both the hardcoded `<|eom|>` entry and the earlier `strip_generic_pipe_tokens()` that incorrectly stripped all occurrences.

workflow: First implementation iterated twice: initial approach added token to hardcoded list + generic everywhere-stripper; user pushed back on both hardcoding and over-broad stripping; final solution uses structural signal (trailing position) rather than pattern-matching all occurrences.
When reverting intermediate changes across multiple files, verify each file individually rather than bulk-reverting to avoid accidentally discarding intentional changes.
The providers crate streaming changes were fully reverted after the design pivot — the final fix is entirely in the agents sanitizer layer.

friction: Two design pivots required: first from hardcoded list to generic pattern, then from generic everywhere-stripping to trailing-only stripping — the correct mental model (structural signal vs. text pattern) wasn't reached until the second user correction.
Pre-existing syntax error in `crates/providers/tests/openai_compat_type_arrays.rs` caused a spurious test failure that had to be diagnosed and ruled out.
Duplicate logic between `response_sanitizer.rs` and the (eventually reverted) `openai_compat/mod.rs` implementation was a consequence of the wrong dependency direction between crates.
open_items: Leaked stop tokens still briefly flash in the streaming UI during the response — the fix only cleans the final stored text. A streaming-layer filter would prevent the flash but requires careful scoping to avoid false positives.

The `STANDALONE_PIPE_TOKENS` list in `response_sanitizer.rs` still contains explicit entries (e.g., `<|eom|>` was added then possibly retained) — worth auditing whether the list is still needed given the generic trailing stripper.
Pre-existing syntax error in `crates/providers/tests/openai_compat_type_arrays.rs:381` (extra `}`) should be fixed separately.
